title: 10 best herbal tinctures for migraine
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for migraine are concentrated liquid extracts of plant-based ingredients, designed to alleviate symptoms of migraines. These tinctures work by soothing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and calming nervous system activity, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraines. Examples include peppermint, feverfew, and passionflower tinctures, which have been shown to provide fast and effective relief from migraine pain. By using herbal tinctures for migraine, individuals can experience reduced suffering, improved quality of life, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with migraine because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenes that work together to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the trigeminal nerve, which is often overactive during migraines. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to migraine pain. By reducing inflammation and relaxing blood vessels, maidenhair tree tinctures can provide natural relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to migraine development.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Ginkgo biloba tincture can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the brain and potentially reducing migraine pain and frequency.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the trigeminal nerve, which is often involved in migraine pathophysiology.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and relaxing vascular smooth muscle.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing migraines.
Seeds: The seeds of the maidenhair tree are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to migraine development.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Grind 30g of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 150ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with migraine because it contains parthenolide, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When taken as a supplement, feverfew tincture can help to reduce the frequency, severity, and duration of migraines by inhibiting the release of pain-producing chemicals in the brain and blood vessels. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory effects may help to relax constricted blood vessels, which is a common migraine trigger. By addressing these underlying causes, feverfew tinctures offer a natural and effective way to manage and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's main active compound, parthenolide, has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light.

Vasoconstriction properties: Feverfew tinctures can cause blood vessels to constrict, which may help reduce the dilation of blood vessels that often occurs during migraines, leading to a reduction in headache severity.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Feverfew's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine development and progression.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Feverfew flowers are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help to alleviate migraine symptoms.

Roots: The roots of the feverfew plant contain a higher concentration of essential oils than other parts, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or air-drying method at room temperature within 24 hours.
Chop dried tanacetum parthenium into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka to achieve a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Store the tincture mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and let it steep for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard solids after 4 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur tinctures helps with migraine because they contain compounds that have a natural ability to calm and relax the brain and blood vessels. The flavonoids and terpenes present in butterbur tincture work together to reduce inflammation, alleviate muscle tension, and ease spasms that can contribute to migraine pain. Additionally, butterbur's neuroprotective properties may help stabilize serotonin levels, which is often imbalanced during migraine episodes, further contributing to its therapeutic effects in reducing frequency and severity of migraines.
remedy_properties: Pain relief: Butterbur's sesquiterpenes, particularly petasin and isopetasin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and block pain pathways in the brain, providing relief from migraine headaches.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Butterbur's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain and symptoms such as sensitivity to light and sound.

Vasoconstriction and vasodilation regulation: Butterbur's sesquiterpenes may help regulate the constriction and dilation of blood vessels, which is thought to be a contributing factor to migraines, and may help to prevent or reduce migraine attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the butterbur plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in treating migraine symptoms.
Leaves: Butterbur leaves are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and relieve migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried petasites hybridus leaves and stems.
Dry the plant material using shade drying or air circulation.
Cut the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area.
Create the tincture by soaking the plant material in vodka.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with migraine because of its unique ability to provide natural pain relief. The salicin present in white willow bark is a gentle, non-narcotic alternative to aspirin that targets the underlying causes of migraines, including inflammation and blood vessel constriction. By reducing pain and discomfort, herbal white willow tinctures can help alleviate symptoms associated with migraines, such as throbbing headaches, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. Regular use of white willow tincture may also reduce frequency and severity of migraine attacks over time.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tincture contains salicin, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce migraine pain by blocking prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause blood vessels to swell and lead to pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in white willow tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are thought to play a role in the development of migraines.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: White willow tincture has been shown to have analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce migraine pain and inflammation in the blood vessels, leading to relief from symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of white willow contain salicin, a natural pain reliever that helps alleviate migraine headaches.
Barks: The bark of white willow is rich in salicin and other bioactive compounds, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather salix alba bark and 80% ethanol or vodka as the solvent ingredient.
Chop one pound of dried salix alba bark into small pieces and mix with one liter of ethanol or vodka.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks in a cool dark place shaking occasionally daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use 20-30 drops as needed up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with migraine because it has a cooling and calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles. The menthol in peppermint also has analgesic properties, which can help to ease headache pain. Additionally, peppermint's ability to improve digestion and relieve nausea can be particularly helpful for those who experience these symptoms during migraine episodes. By addressing these underlying triggers, herbal peppermint tinctures can provide effective relief from migraine discomfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive relief: Peppermint tincture's ability to ease digestive issues like IBS, nausea, and stomach discomfort can alleviate migraine triggers caused by gut dysbiosis.

Pain relief: The menthol in peppermint tincture has a natural analgesic effect, helping to reduce migraine pain and inflammation by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are known pain mediators.
Vasodilation and improved circulation: Peppermint tincture's vasodilating properties help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing tension that can contribute to migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are rich in menthol, which helps to ease headache and migraine pain by relaxing the muscles and improving blood flow.
Buds: Peppermint buds contain high levels of menthone, a natural analgesic that can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, tension, and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a pesticide-free garden at dawn for maximum potency.
Rinse the peppermint leaves gently with cool distilled water and pat dry with paper towels immediately.
Combine one part of dried or 10 parts of fresh peppermint leaves with 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a dark place at room temperature, shaking daily to enhance extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean amber glass bottle for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels. The bioactive compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to relax the smooth muscles in the blood vessels, reducing their constriction and dilation, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, ginger has natural analgesic and anti-nausea properties, providing relief from migraine-related pain and discomfort. Regular use of herbal ginger tinctures can significantly reduce frequency and severity of migraines, offering a natural and effective remedy for those who suffer from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in ginger, such as 6-gingerol, can help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing constriction, which is thought to be a trigger for migraines.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage to blood vessels, all of which are potential migraine triggers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are a popular choice for making ginger tinctures due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms.

Roots: Ginger roots contain a wealth of bioactive compounds like gingerols and sesquiterpenes, which are effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with migraines when used in tincture form.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried zingiber officinale rhizomes from a trusted source weighing 20 ounces.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and mix with 80% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar filling it up to 4 inches from the top.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4 weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the tincture to steep.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and rinsing the filter with 1 ounce of vodka or grain alcohol.
Label the final product clearly and store it in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 2 years taking 20-30 drops as needed before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with migraine because its unique combination of bioactive compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, has a profound impact on the body's response to pain. The herb has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often triggers migraines. Additionally, valerian tinctures have been shown to improve sleep quality, another common migraine trigger. By addressing these underlying factors, valerian tinctures may offer natural relief from migraine symptoms, reducing frequency and severity of episodes.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian root has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can help alleviate migraine pain and tension.

GABA-enhancing properties: Valerian root is a natural GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) agonist, which helps regulate the body's response to stress and anxiety, reducing the frequency and severity of migraines.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: Valerian root has antioxidant compounds that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in volatile oils and alkaloids, which are responsible for its calming and relaxing properties, making them effective for soothing migraine symptoms.
Barks: The barks of valerian contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valerian roots from a reputable source in amounts of 100-200 grams for large batches.
Chop the harvested valerian roots into small pieces to ensure even extraction during the maceration process.
Combine the chopped valerian root pieces with 80% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a glass jar covering them by at least two inches.
Allow the mixture to steep and macerate for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate solvent penetration.
Strain the resulting tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids and storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with migraine because it contains passionflower, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to alleviate anxiety and insomnia, which are common triggers for migraines. The tincture's sedative properties help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and tension that can contribute to migraine development. Additionally, maypop tinctures contain flavonoids and alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief from headache pain and discomfort. By addressing both physical and emotional triggers, maypop tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing migraines.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maypop tincture helps to relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, which can alleviate migraine pain caused by blood vessel constriction.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The tincture's calming properties can help to reduce stress, anxiety, and tension, common migraine triggers, promoting relaxation and reducing the frequency and severity of migraines.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions: Maypop contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine development and progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective for migraine relief.
Barks: Maypop barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation, relax blood vessels, and ease tension headaches associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passiflora incarnata flowers in amounts of 2 cups for every 100 milliliters of tincture desired.
Combine harvested passiflora incarnata flowers with 70% ethyl alcohol in a clean glass jar to create the tincture base.
Allow the mixture to steep and macerate for 4-6 weeks in a dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the plant material.
Store the finished tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label accordingly, using within 2-3 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with migraine because its calming properties can alleviate tension and stress, common triggers of migraines. The tincture's natural anti-inflammatory compounds can also reduce swelling in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine pain. Additionally, the gentle sedative effects of English lavender can promote relaxation and help regulate sleep patterns, which are often disrupted by migraine episodes. By soothing the mind and body, English lavender tinctures can provide effective relief from migraines, reducing frequency and severity.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Lavender's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common migraine triggers, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of migraines.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help alleviate headache pain, inflammation, and discomfort associated with migraines.

Sedative and calming effects: The sedative compounds in lavender, particularly linalool, can promote relaxation, reduce muscle tension, and calm the nervous system, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms like dizziness, sensitivity to light and sound, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to relax muscles and calm nervous tension associated with migraines.
Flowers: Known for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, flowers soothe headaches and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities of 200-250 grams.
Combine the herb material with high-proof grain alcohol in a ratio of 1:3 parts by weight.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass containers.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles and label as a migraine relief remedy using 20-30 drops per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with migraine because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing inflammation and tension that can contribute to migraine pain. The apigenin present in chamomile interacts with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and easing muscle contractions that can exacerbate migraines. Additionally, chamomile's antispasmodic properties help to relax blood vessels, which may reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks. By calming the body and mind, herbal chamomile tinctures provide natural relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) property: Chamomile's anxiolytic effect helps reduce stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers, which can alleviate migraine symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the brain, a potential cause of migraines, and ease associated pain and discomfort.
Muscle relaxant property: Chamomile's muscle relaxant properties can help soothe tense muscles, including those involved in migraine episodes, which can lead to reduced pain and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used to make a calming and sedative tincture that can help ease migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory tincture that can help reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in bulk for processing.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or dehydrator at low heat for several hours.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with four parts vodka or 80 proof rum and macerate for two weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard solids after several minutes.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least six weeks before using as directed to help with migraine relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with migraine because its active compounds, including taraxasterol and triterpenoid acids, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These bioactive molecules work to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate the body's natural detoxification processes helps eliminate toxins and heavy metals that can contribute to migraine attacks. By targeting both inflammatory and toxic triggers, herbal dandelion tinctures provide a natural and effective way to alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are thought to contribute to migraine development and severity.

Choleretic and hepatoprotective effects: Dandelion's ability to stimulate bile production and protect the liver may help alleviate migraines by improving liver function and reducing toxicity in the body.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Dandelion's flavonoids, such as quercetin, have been shown to exhibit sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing migraine pain and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called sesquiterpene lactone, which has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh dandelion leaves and flowers in the morning after dew.
Dry the harvested material for 2 hours in a warm, dark place to preserve potency.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250ml of 70% ethanol with 50g of the powdered taraxacum officinale and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with migraine because it contains isoflavones, particularly biochanin A, which has been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby alleviating migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. Additionally, red clover tinctures may also help to relax vascular smooth muscle, reducing the frequency and severity of migraines by modulating vascular tone.
remedy_properties: Isolation of Isovolemic Flavonoids: Red clover tincture contains isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, which have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of migraine-related vasospasm.

Antioxidant Activity: The flavonoid-rich composition of red clover tincture exhibits strong antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with migraines.

Vasodilatory Effects: Red clover's isoflavones also exhibit vasodilatory effects, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain, potentially reducing migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving migraine pain.

Flowers: The flowers of the red clover plant are rich in isoflavones, which have been found to have a beneficial effect on blood vessels and may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest trifolium pratense flowers at peak bloom, ensuring optimal potency for medicinal purposes.
Weigh and clean the harvested flowers, removing any debris or contaminants from the processing area.
Combine one part of the cleaned trifolium pratense flowers with five parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture for use as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with migraine because it has a profound impact on the body's ability to relax and reduce anxiety, which are common triggers for migraines. The herb contains compounds that have a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate feelings of tension and stress. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, skullcap tinctures can help to prevent migraines from occurring or reduce their severity when they do happen, providing much-needed relief from debilitating headaches.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: Skullcap's flavonoids and terpenes reduce pain and inflammation, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms such as headache pain and sensitivity to light.
Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Skullcap's compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may trigger or exacerbate migraines.
Antispasmodic effects: Skullcap's flavonoids and alkaloids help relax smooth muscle tissue, including blood vessels, which may help alleviate migraine-related vasospasm and reduce headache severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are believed to be responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving migraines.
Rhyzomes: Skullcap rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat headaches, including migraines, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scutellaria lateriflora leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried organic material in sufficient quantity for preparation.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and combine with vodka at a 1:2 ratio by weight to create a maceration mixture.
Place the maceration mixture in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid tincture.
Transfer the reserved tincture to smaller dark glass bottles and label them with the date of preparation and ingredients used for future use at 10-20 drops per serving twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the blood vessels. The herb's flavonoids and hyperforin work together to inhibit the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters, providing relief from migraine symptoms. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to improve mood and reduce stress levels, common triggers for migraines. By addressing these underlying factors, St John's Wort tinctures can help alleviate migraine frequency, duration, and severity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are thought to contribute to migraine development.
Pain relief: The hyperforin present in St John's Wort has been shown to have analgesic properties, making it helpful for relieving headache pain associated with migraines.

Vasodilation and blood flow regulation: St John's Wort's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing vasoconstriction (narrowing of blood vessels) that can lead to migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a concentrated extract that can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain.

Flowers: The bright yellow flowers contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a key compound responsible for the plant's antidepressant and analgesic properties, making them an effective part for treating migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers at peak blooming in morning dew for optimal potency.
Cleanse and dry the harvested flowers by air drying or gentle heat to prevent mold growth.
Chop 10 grams of dried st john's wort flowers into small pieces and combine with 100ml of high-proof vodka or rum.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar at room temperature for 14 days, shaking daily to extract essential oils.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with migraine because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The silymarin compound found in milk thistle has been shown to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a common contributor to migraines. Additionally, milk thistle's ability to detoxify the liver and brain can help remove toxins that may trigger or exacerbate migraine symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting detoxification, herbal milk thistle tinctures can provide natural relief from migraine pain and frequency.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to migraine development. This helps to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in milk thistle tinctures have antioxidant properties that help protect the body against free radicals, which can trigger migraines. By neutralizing these radicals, flavonoids may help prevent or reduce migraine attacks.

Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to ease headache pain and discomfort associated with migraines. They also exhibit vasoactive effects, which may help to dilate blood vessels and alleviate migraine-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain silybin, a bioactive compound that has been found to have potent neuroprotective effects, which can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum leaves and flowers in their peak potency during the summer season.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for at least 48 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with vodka or high-proof grain alcohol at a ratio of 1 part plant to 4 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with migraine because it has a unique ability to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for migraines. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stressful situations, reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation. Additionally, ashwagandha has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the brain and nervous system, which is thought to contribute to migraine pain. By addressing these underlying causes of migraines, ashwagandha tinctures can provide relief from frequent and debilitating attacks.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which can trigger or exacerbate migraines, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to migraine symptoms.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Ashwagandha's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related migraines by promoting relaxation and reducing tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds, making them a primary source for medicinal extracts that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.

Barks: The bark of the ashwagandha plant contains a high concentration of withanolides, which have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ashwagandha root and leaf material in early morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by gently brushing off dirt and debris thoroughly.
Chop the clean plant material into small pieces and measure out 20-30 grams per cup of menstruum solvent.
Combine the chopped plant material with 100 proof vodka or isopropyl alcohol in a glass jar, covering the contents completely.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks, shaking daily, before straining and filtering the final tincture product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with migraine because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in blood vessels and alleviate pressure on the trigeminal nerve. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-spasmodic activities, which may help to relax tense muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This property helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are thought to contribute to migraine pain and frequency.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing blood vessel constriction and oxidative damage.

Isoflavans: Isoflavans found in licorice root have been demonstrated to have a direct effect on the nervous system, potentially helping to regulate pain pathways and reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used in migraine tinctures due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoids and saponins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful in treating migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when mature.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root with 2 parts 95% ethanol vodka in a glass jar, then store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass bottle, discard the solids and label the tincture as glycyrrhiza glabra extract 1:2 25% solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with migraine because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties, which can effectively alleviate pain and discomfort associated with migraines. The active compounds in yarrow, such as acetic acid and chromenes, work to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which may contribute to the development of migraines. Additionally, yarrow's ability to stimulate the body's natural healing processes can help to regulate hormonal imbalances, another common migraine trigger. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal yarrow tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing and reducing migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a common trigger for migraine headaches.
Vasoconstrictive: The vasoconstrictive properties of yarrow tincture help constrict dilated blood vessels, thereby reducing the pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
Antioxidant: Yarrow tincture's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, a known migraine trigger.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help relieve migraines.
Buds: Containing volatile oils and flavonoids, the buds are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, making them effective for migraine relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried a. millefolium leaves and flowers weighing about one ounce.
Combine the herb material with vodka as a solvent in a clean glass jar filling it two-thirds full.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it at room temperature for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tinctures helps with migraine because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and omega-6 fatty acids. The herb's active ingredients, including boric acid and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is often a trigger for migraines. Additionally, borage tinctures' potent antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to migraine pain and inflammation. By addressing these underlying factors, borage tinctures provide relief from migraine symptoms, offering a natural and effective way to manage this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Borage tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) and oleic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate migraine symptoms.

Pain relief: The tincture's active compounds, including sesquiterpenes and polyphenols, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, helping to relieve pain associated with migraines.
Vasoactive effects: Borage tincture's flavonoids and phenolic acids can help regulate blood vessel tone, reducing the frequency and severity of migraines caused by vasodilation or vasoconstriction.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the borage plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with migraines.
Seeds: Borage seeds contain a compound called gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has been shown to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried borago officinalis flowers in amounts of one ounce per pint of menstruum.
Combine harvested material with high-proof vodka or neutral grain spirit at a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking daily and monitoring temperature below 80 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard solids immediately.
Label and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place with a recommended dosage of 20-30 drops twice daily.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal tinctures are tension headaches, cluster headaches, and sinusitis. Herbs like Feverfew, Willow Bark, and Ginger have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in alleviating pain and reducing inflammation. Tinctures of these herbs can be used topically or taken orally to provide relief from headache symptoms such as throbbing pain, sensitivity to light, and nausea.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat migraine with herbal tinctures is to combine a blend of botanicals that have been traditionally used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. For example, a mix of Willow Bark, Feverfew, and Skullcap can be effective in reducing headache frequency and severity. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture three times daily as needed, or as directed by a healthcare practitioner.
